headline: Stock market posts first outflow since March
detail: Money is rotating out of equities for the first time in months, signaling institutional caution heading into summer. For your concentrated tech/chip book on margin, outflows hit high-beta names hardest — AVGO, PLTR, and CRWD are most exposed.

headline: Earnings exuberance becoming a problem, says Wall Street veteran
detail: A veteran strategist is warning that the market is pricing perfection into earnings, leaving no cushion for misses. AVGO and PLTR are high-expectation names — any guidance softness could accelerate your existing losses.

headline: Oil falls below $70, could drop to sub-$50
detail: Falling oil is a macro signal of softening global demand, which historically pressures risk appetite broadly. It won't directly hit your chip names, but a risk-off macro mood adds selling pressure to the whole growth-tech space you're concentrated in.

headline: Warsh reshaping Fed advisory appointments
detail: Personnel moves at the Fed can shift rate-cut expectations — higher-for-longer odds are a direct negative for high-multiple tech stocks like PLTR and AVGO that depend on cheap money for their valuations.
